name: flit-core

metadata.name: flit-core
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: Distribution-building parts of Flit. See flit package for more information

documentation:
# flit_core

This provides a [PEP 517](https://peps.python.org/pep-0517/) build backend
for packages using [Flit](https://pypi.org/project/flit/). The only public
interface is the API specified by PEP 517, at `flit_core.buildapi`.

See the [Flit documentation](https://flit.pypa.io/en/stable/) for more
information.

modules.name: flit_core
documentation:
Flit's core machinery for building packages.

This package provides a standard PEP 517 API to build packages using Flit.
All the convenient development features live in the main 'flit' package.

modules.name: flit_core.versionno
documentation: Normalise version number according to PEP 440

functions.name: flit_core.versionno.normalise_version
documentation:
Normalise version number according to rules in PEP 440

Raises InvalidVersion if the version does not match PEP 440. This can be
overridden with the FLIT_ALLOW_INVALID environment variable.

https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0440/#normalization

functions.name: flit_core.sdist.clean_tarinfo
documentation:
Clean metadata from a TarInfo object to make it more reproducible.

- Set uid & gid to 0
- Set uname and gname to ""
- Normalise permissions to 644 or 755
- Set mtime if not None

classes.name: flit_core.sdist.FilePatterns
documentation: Manage a set of file inclusion/exclusion patterns relative to basedir

classes.name: flit_core.sdist.SdistBuilder

methods.name: flit_core.sdist.SdistBuilder.select_files
documentation:
Pick which files from the source tree will be included in the sdist

This is overridden in flit itself to use information from a VCS to
include tests, docs, etc. for a 'gold standard' sdist.

methods.name: flit_core.sdist.SdistBuilder.add_setup_py
documentation: No-op here; overridden in flit to generate setup.py

Builds a minimal sdist

These minimal sdists should work for PEP 517.
The class is extended in flit.sdist to make a more 'full fat' sdist,
which is what should normally be published to PyPI.

modules.name: flit_core.vendor.tomli
documentation: A lil' TOML parser.

variables.name: flit_core.vendor.tomli.__version__
documentation: DO NOT EDIT THIS LINE MANUALLY. LET bump2version UTILITY DO IT

functions.name: flit_core.common._module_load_ctx
documentation:
Preserve some global state that modules might change at import time.

- Handlers on the root logger.

functions.name: flit_core.common.get_docstring_and_version_via_ast
documentation:
Return a tuple like (docstring, version) for the given module,
extracted by parsing its AST.

functions.name: flit_core.common.get_docstring_and_version_via_import
documentation:
Return a tuple like (docstring, version) for the given module,
extracted by importing the module and pulling __doc__ & __version__
from it.

functions.name: flit_core.common.get_info_from_module
documentation: Load the module/package, get its docstring and __version__

functions.name: flit_core.common.check_version
documentation:
Check whether a given version string match PEP 440, and do normalisation.

Raise InvalidVersion/NoVersionError with relevant information if
version is invalid.

Log a warning if the version is not canonical with respect to PEP 440.

Returns the version in canonical PEP 440 format.

functions.name: flit_core.common.parse_entry_point
documentation:
Check and parse a 'package.module:func' style entry point specification.

Returns (modulename, funcname)

functions.name: flit_core.common.write_entry_points
documentation:
Write entry_points.txt from a two-level dict

Sorts on keys to ensure results are reproducible.

functions.name: flit_core.common.normalize_file_permissions
documentation:
Normalize the permission bits in the st_mode field from stat to 644/755

Popular VCSs only track whether a file is executable or not. The exact
permissions can vary on systems with different umasks. Normalising
to 644 (non executable) or 755 (executable) makes builds more reproducible.

functions.name: flit_core.common.normalise_core_metadata_name
documentation: Normalise a project or extra name (as in PEP 503, also PEP 685)

functions.name: flit_core.common.normalize_dist_name
documentation:
Normalizes a name and a PEP 440 version

The resulting string is valid as dist-info folder name
and as first part of a wheel filename

See https://packaging.python.org/specifications/binary-distribution-format/#escaping-and-unicode

functions.name: flit_core.common.dist_info_name
documentation: Get the correct name of the .dist-info folder

functions.name: flit_core.common.walk_data_dir
documentation:
Iterate over the files in the given data directory.

Yields paths prefixed with data_directory - caller may want to make them
relative to that. Excludes any __pycache__ subdirectories.

classes.name: flit_core.common.Module

methods.name: flit_core.common.Module.version_files
documentation:
Files which will be parsed to find a version number

Files later in this list take precedence over earlier ones.

methods.name: flit_core.common.Module.iter_files
documentation:
Iterate over the files contained in this module.

Yields absolute paths - caller may want to make them relative.
Excludes any __pycache__ and *.pyc files.
This represents the module/package that we are going to distribute

methods.name: flit_core.common.Metadata.write_metadata_file
documentation: Write out metadata in the email headers format

methods.name: flit_core.common.Metadata.supports_py2
documentation: Return True if Requires-Python indicates Python 2 support.

functions.name: flit_core.vendor.tomli._re.match_to_datetime
documentation:
Convert a `RE_DATETIME` match to `datetime.datetime` or `datetime.date`.

Raises ValueError if the match does not correspond to a valid date
or datetime.

modules.name: flit_core.buildapi
documentation: PEP-517 compliant buildsystem API

functions.name: flit_core.buildapi.get_requires_for_build_wheel
documentation: Returns a list of requirements for building, as strings

functions.name: flit_core.buildapi.prepare_metadata_for_build_wheel
documentation: Creates {metadata_directory}/foo-1.2.dist-info

functions.name: flit_core.buildapi.build_wheel
documentation: Builds a wheel, places it in wheel_directory

functions.name: flit_core.buildapi.build_editable
documentation: Builds an "editable" wheel, places it in wheel_directory

functions.name: flit_core.buildapi.build_sdist
documentation: Builds an sdist, places it in sdist_directory

functions.name: flit_core.wheel.zip_timestamp_from_env
documentation: Prepare a timestamp from $SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH, if set

methods.name: flit_core.wheel.WheelBuilder.__init__
documentation: Build a wheel from a module/package

functions.name: flit_core.vendor.tomli._parser.load
documentation: Parse TOML from a binary file object.

functions.name: flit_core.vendor.tomli._parser.loads
documentation: Parse TOML from a string.

functions.name: flit_core.vendor.tomli._parser.suffixed_err
documentation:
Return a `TOMLDecodeError` where error message is suffixed with
coordinates in source.

classes.name: flit_core.vendor.tomli._parser.TOMLDecodeError
documentation: An error raised if a document is not valid TOML.

classes.name: flit_core.vendor.tomli._parser.Flags
documentation: Flags that map to parsed keys/namespaces.

functions.name: flit_core.config.read_flit_config
documentation: Read and check the `pyproject.toml` file with data about the package.

functions.name: flit_core.config.prep_toml_config
documentation:
Validate config loaded from pyproject.toml and prepare common metadata

Returns a LoadedConfig object.

functions.name: flit_core.config.flatten_entrypoints
documentation:
Flatten nested entrypoints dicts.

Entry points group names can include dots. But dots in TOML make nested
dictionaries:

[entrypoints.a.b]    # {'entrypoints': {'a': {'b': {}}}}

The proper way to avoid this is:

[entrypoints."a.b"]  # {'entrypoints': {'a.b': {}}}

But since there isn't a need for arbitrarily nested mappings in entrypoints,
flit allows you to use the former. This flattens the nested dictionaries
from loading pyproject.toml.

functions.name: flit_core.config._check_glob_patterns
documentation: Check and normalise glob patterns for sdist include/exclude

functions.name: flit_core.config._prep_metadata
documentation:
Process & verify the metadata from a config file

- Pull out the module name we're packaging.
- Read description-file and check that it's valid rst
- Convert dashes in key names to underscores
  (e.g. home-page in config -> home_page in metadata)

functions.name: flit_core.config.pep621_people
documentation: Convert authors/maintainers from PEP 621 to core metadata fields

functions.name: flit_core.config.isabs_ish
documentation:
Like os.path.isabs(), but Windows paths from a drive root count as absolute

isabs() worked this way up to Python 3.12 (inclusive), and where we reject
absolute paths, we also want to reject these odd halfway paths.
